steps: Go to https://secure.backblaze.com/user_signin.htm and sign in to your Backblaze account
In the left navigation under 'B2 Cloud Storage', click 'Application Keys'
Enter a key name, optionally restrict to specific buckets or file prefixes, and set an expiry if needed
Copy both the keyID and the applicationKey immediately — the applicationKey is shown only once
For API calls, use Basic auth with keyID as the username and applicationKey as the password (base64 encoded)

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Application Key (base64 keyID:applicationKey)
credential_fields.description: Base64-encoded string of 'keyID:applicationKey', sent as 'Authorization: Basic <encoded>'. First call b2_authorize_account to obtain a session token and API URL.

notes: The applicationKey is displayed only once at creation — copy it before closing the dialog
You must call /b2api/v3/b2_authorize_account first to get a session token and the correct API URL for subsequent calls
Free tier: 10 GB storage and 1 GB/day downloads free
Pricing is approximately $0.006/GB/month for storage, much lower than AWS S3
Master application key is available on the Account tab; creating separate app keys per use case is recommended

test_endpoint.description: Authorize your account to get a session token and API URL
